query:
INSERT INTO ai_briefs (sourceType, sourceId, sourceName, briefType, content, generatedAt) VALUES
('workspace', 1, 'Collins v. Robinhood Financial LLC — FINRA No. 25-02589', 'internal_case',
JSON_OBJECT(
  'executiveSummary', 'Collins v. Robinhood Financial LLC (FINRA No. 25-02589) is an active arbitration case involving $1,289 in unauthorized USDC withdrawals from the Claimant''s Robinhood account on September 9, 2025. Forensic analysis of Robinhood''s own Exhibit B reveals two previously unseen IP addresses on September 9 — including IP 89.116.218.6 with a generic "United States" location tag that appears nowhere else in the account history. Robinhood''s defense rests entirely on device token hash matching, which proves device use but not Claimant authorization. A Motion to Compel discovery is due TODAY (07/17/2026) and must be filed immediately. Robinhood has rejected a $5,500 counteroffer and is holding at $800 — a nuisance settlement that should be rejected. The case is strong on forensic grounds but requires the wallet approval records Robinhood is withholding.',
  'currentProgress', 'Forensic analysis is complete. Deliverables A (Numerical Summary), B (IP Pattern Table), and C (September 9 Timeline) have been produced. Exhibits 1, 2, and 3 are organized and converted to PDF. The discovery dispute has been fully documented. Robinhood''s procedural error in citing FINRA Rule 12507(a)(1) instead of 12507(a)(3) has been identified and will anchor the Motion to Compel. Settlement negotiations have failed — Robinhood at $800, Claimant at $5,500.',
  'risks', '1. TIMING RISK: Motion to Compel deadline is 07/17/2026 — today. Failure to file today may waive the right to compel discovery. 2. DEVICE TOKEN DEFENSE: Robinhood''s device token argument is legally coherent even if factually incomplete. Must be rebutted with specific precedent. 3. DISCOVERY DENIAL RISK: If Motion to Compel is denied, the wallet approval records — the single most important missing piece — may never be produced. 4. BURDEN OF PROOF: Claimant bears the burden of proving unauthorized access. The forensic evidence is strong but circumstantial without the wallet approval records.',
  'opportunities', '1. Robinhood''s procedural error (wrong FINRA rule subsection) is a strong procedural hook for the Motion to Compel. 2. The "United States" generic location tag on September 9 is unique in the entire account history — powerful visual evidence for an arbitrator. 3. The 10-second gap between a cancelled and successful sell order at 6:49 AM is consistent with automated unauthorized access, not human behavior. 4. If wallet approval records show the USDC destination was never approved by Claimant, the case becomes significantly stronger and settlement value increases.',
  'recommendedNextActions', '1. FILE Motion to Compel on FINRA DR Portal TODAY — include all exhibits and forensic deliverables. 2. UPLOAD complete evidence bundle to FINRA DR Portal alongside the motion. 3. DRAFT rebuttal brief for Robinhood device token defense — due 07/20/2026. 4. DEMAND wallet approval records for USDC destination address as primary discovery target. 5. RESEARCH FINRA arbitration precedents on unauthorized access where broker denied liability based on device/session matching.',
  'missingInformation', '1. Wallet approval records for the USDC destination address used on September 9 — critical missing piece. 2. Internal Robinhood fraud investigation notes or flags triggered during the 6:49 AM liquidation. 3. Full MFA/2FA authentication logs for September 9 sessions. 4. Device approval records — when was the device token created and from what IP address. 5. Whether Robinhood''s security systems flagged the generic "United States" location tag in real time.'
),
NOW());
